message.content:
Design an implementation plan for improving the K3RN Platform's dossiers listing page (/workspace) based on user feedback. The codebase is a Next.js App Router project with Tailwind CSS and Radix UI components.

## User Requirements

### 1. Design/Contrast Improvements
The user says the page is too dark with accent colors that are "trop rangées" (too harsh/stark), creating too much contrast. They want more depth and nuance.

Currently the page uses:
- `bg-background` (pure black #000000) as page background
- `bg-[--color-bg-card]` (#1A1A1A) for cards
- `#F75105` (bright orange) as accent
- `#D2D3D4` and `#5D5C5B` for text

The issue is: dark black + bright orange = too stark. Need intermediate surface layers and softer accent usage.

### 2. Dossier Settings (NOT entering the laboratory)
Users need to manage dossier metadata without opening the full workspace:
- Rename the dossier (title)
- Add a description
- Review scores/decision
- Set a color tag
- This should be accessible from the card (e.g., via the MoreVertical menu on list view, or a gear icon)

### 3. Filtering/Sorting System
Filter and sort dossiers by:
- Decision (GO/EXPLORE/PARK/KILL)
- Score range
- Date
- Potentially by color tag

### 4. Contextual Header Navigation
The SiteHeader currently shows "Laboratoire" on all app pages. The user wants contextual navigation in the header that changes based on the current page:
- On /workspace: show "Dossiers" or breadcrumb context
- On /workspace/[id]: show "Laboratoire" or project name
- On /capture: show "Capture" or analysis context

## Available UI Components (already exist in src/components/ui/)
- Dialog (Radix UI) - for dossier settings modal
- DropdownMenu (Radix UI) - for sort options, context menus
- Select (Radix UI) - for filter dropdowns
- Sheet (Radix UI) - for side panel
- Input, Button, Badge, Tabs, Checkbox, Switch, Label
- DecisionBadge - for GO/EXPLORE/PARK/KILL badges

## Current File Structure
- `src/app/workspace/page.tsx` - Dossiers listing page (main target)
- `src/components/layout/site-header.tsx` - Header component (needs contextual nav)
- `src/app/globals.css` - CSS design tokens
- `src/lib/i18n/dictionary.ts` - i18n (FR/EN/ES)
- `src/store/language-store.ts` - Zustand store for language

## Design System Tokens (from globals.css)
```
--color-bg-primary: #000000
--color-bg-secondary: #0F0F0F
--color-bg-card: #1A1A1A
--color-bg-card-hover: #22242B
--color-surface-1: #2F323A
--color-surface-2: #5D5C5B
--color-border-subtle: rgba(255,255,255,0.05)
--color-border-default: rgba(255,255,255,0.1)
--color-border-strong: rgba(255,255,255,0.2)
--color-text-primary: #FFFFFF
--color-text-secondary: #D2D3D4
--color-text-muted: #5D5C5B
--color-brand-primary: #F75105
--color-brand-secondary: #F78E21
--radius-2xl: 24px (standard card radius)
--shadow-glow: 0 0 30px rgba(247, 81, 5, 0.3)
```

## Current workspace/page.tsx structure
- Project interface with id, title, text, scores, recommendation, timestamp, isDemo, analysis
- Grid view (3 columns) with "New Project" card + project cards
- List view with rows
- Search bar
- View toggle (grid/list)
- Uses SiteHeader variant="app"
- Helper functions: getProjectScore, getProjectDecision, getProjectTitle
- Loads from localStorage, falls back to mock data

## Key Constraints
- Keep using existing design tokens and component library
- Don't over-engineer - keep it practical
- The Project interface stored in localStorage needs to be extended (add color, description)
- The dossier settings should use Dialog component
- Filtering should be inline (not a separate page)

Please provide a detailed, concrete implementation plan with specific file changes, new components needed, and the approach for each feature.

content.text: I'll start by thoroughly exploring the codebase to understand the current implementation, then design a detailed plan for each requirement.
Now let me explore the existing UI components and other key files.

Here is the detailed implementation plan. --- # K3RN Platform Workspace Improvements -- Implementation Plan ## Overview of Current State After thorough exploration of the codebase, here is what exists today: - **`/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/app/workspace/page.tsx`** -- The dossiers listing page. A 245-line client component with a `Project` interface, grid/list views, search bar, and view toggle. Data is loaded from `localStorage` under key `k3rn_projects`. - **`/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/layout/site-header.tsx`** -- 75 lines. Has two variants: `"public"` and `"app"`. In app mode it shows the K3RN logo + hardcoded label "Laboratoire" and user controls. No contextual awareness of current route. - **`/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/app/globals.css`** -- Design tokens. The page background is `#000000`, card is `#1A1A1A`, brand accent is `#F75105`. There are intermediate surfaces (`#0F0F0F`, `#2F323A`) that exist as tokens but are NOT used on the workspace page. - **`/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/i18n/dictionary.ts`** -- Full FR/EN/ES dictionary. The `workspace` section has limited keys; it lacks labels for filtering, sorting, settings, and contextual nav. - **`/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/workspace/header.tsx`** -- A separate `WorkspaceHeader` used only on `/workspace/[id]` (the laboratory page), distinct from `SiteHeader`. The UI component library at `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/ui/` includes: `dialog.tsx`, `dropdown-menu.tsx`, `select.tsx`, `input.tsx`, `textarea.tsx`, `badge.tsx`, `button.tsx`, `slider.tsx`, `tabs.tsx`, `sheet.tsx`, `checkbox.tsx`, `switch.tsx`, `label.tsx`, `decision-badge.tsx`. --- ## FEATURE 1: Design & Contrast Improvements ### Problem Analysis The current workspace page uses: - Page wrapper: `bg-background` which resolves to `--color-bg-primary: #000000` - Cards: `bg-[--color-bg-card]` which is `#1A1A1A` - Direct orange references: `#F75105` used inline everywhere (hover borders, icon transitions, glow shadows, text hovers) - The gap between `#000000` (page) and `#1A1A1A` (card) is only 26 brightness steps -- cards barely lift off the background The intermediate surfaces that already exist as tokens are NOT being used: - `--color-bg-secondary: #0F0F0F` -- designed for section backgrounds - `--color-bg-card-hover: #22242B` -- card hover state - `--color-surface-1: #2F323A` -- interactive surfaces ### Approach: Layered Surface Hierarchy The solution does NOT require changing the design tokens (they are marked "Contractual -- Non-negotiable"). Instead, we introduce intermediate surface layers to create visual depth. ### Step 1: Add new tokens to `globals.css` Add two new optional ambient tokens for the workspace specifically (these complement, not replace, the canonical tokens): ```css /* In globals.css :root, after existing tokens */ --color-brand-primary-soft: rgba(247, 81, 5, 0.06); /* Ambient orange tint */ --color-brand-primary-muted: rgba(247, 81, 5, 0.15); /* Softer accent for borders */ --shadow-card-subtle: 0 2px 12px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4), 0 0 0 1px rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.03); --shadow-card-hover: 0 8px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5), 0 0 20px rgba(247, 81, 5, 0.08); ``` ### Step 2: Modify `workspace/page.tsx` surface layers **Page background** -- Change from flat black to the secondary surface: ```tsx // BEFORE:
// AFTER:
``` This moves the page from `#000000` to `#0F0F0F` -- a subtle but meaningful lift. **Card backgrounds** -- Add a subtle inner glow and use the card-hover token: ```tsx // BEFORE: className="... bg-[--color-bg-card] ... hover:border-[#F75105]/30 shadow-card hover:shadow-[0_0_30px_rgba(247,81,5,0.15)] ..." // AFTER: className="... bg-[--color-bg-card] ... hover:border-[--color-brand-primary-muted] shadow-[--shadow-card-subtle] hover:shadow-[--shadow-card-hover] hover:bg-[--color-bg-card-hover] ..." ``` **Orange accent softening** -- Replace all hardcoded `#F75105` references with softer usage: | Current (stark) | Replacement (nuanced) | |---|---| | `hover:border-[#F75105]/30` | `hover:border-white/15` (or `hover:border-[--color-brand-primary-muted]`) | | `group-hover:text-[#F75105]` on card titles | `group-hover:text-[--color-brand-secondary]` (uses `#F78E21`, warmer/softer orange) | | `group-hover:bg-[#F75105]` on Plus icon | `group-hover:bg-[--color-brand-primary]/80` (reduce opacity) | | `hover:shadow-[0_0_30px_rgba(247,81,5,0.15)]` | `hover:shadow-[--shadow-card-hover]` (subtler glow) | | `focus:border-[#F75105]/50` on search | `focus:border-[--color-brand-secondary]/40` | **Score display** -- Currently scores above 70 are `text-white`, below are `text-[#5D5C5B]`.
